source: Cadre national de qualification des incidents de cybersecurite et de cybermenaces nationales majeures (HCPN v1.0, 22.08.2025)

essential_services.description: CER essential services (EU Delegated Regulation 2023/2450) — Criterion 1 reference list. Extensible by competent authorities, CERC, or CC.
essential_services.note: Scope is extensible — competent authorities, CERC, or CC may extend to any service whose disruption would harm vital interests, national security, public order, economic stability, or country continuity.

incident_levels.label: Large-scale cybersecurity incident
incident_levels.description: Disruptions exceeding Luxembourg's response capacity OR significant impact on at least one other Member State/third country

incident_levels.label: National major incident
incident_levels.description: Disruptions prejudicial to vital interests or essential needs of all or part of the country/population, requiring urgent decisions and coordination

incident_levels.label: Important incident
incident_levels.description: Serious operational disruption or financial losses for the entity; may affect other persons causing material/physical/moral damage

incident_levels.label: Incident
incident_levels.description: Compromise of CIA+A of data or services

threat_levels.label: Large-scale cyber threat
threat_levels.description: Threat that could cause disruptions exceeding Luxembourg's response capacity OR significant potential impact on another Member State/third country

threat_levels.label: National major cyber threat
threat_levels.description: Threat that could cause disruptions prejudicial to vital interests or essential needs

threat_levels.label: Important cyber threat
threat_levels.description: Technical characteristics suggest grave potential impact

threat_levels.label: Cyber threat
threat_levels.description: Any potential circumstance/event/action capable of harming networks/information systems

threat_probability_levels.description: Criterion 2 for threats — only High and Imminent qualify

levels.label: Imminent
levels.description: About to materialise, immediate response required

criterion_2_sub_criteria.description: Prejudice to vital interests — at least one must be satisfied

sub_criteria.label: Users affected
sub_criteria.description: Affects substantial portion of national population or significant number of critical entities
sub_criteria.note: Defined by sectoral authorities — no quantitative value in framework

sub_criteria.label: Geographic spread
sub_criteria.description: Simultaneous effects across significant geographic area, or cross-border propagation potential

sub_criteria.label: Service interruption
sub_criteria.description: Total interruption of essential service, OR severe degradation over significant duration
sub_criteria.note: Total interruption is deterministic; 'significant duration' defined by sectoral authorities

sub_criteria.label: Human impact
sub_criteria.description: At least one death, OR serious injuries/health harm to multiple individuals

sub_criteria.label: Economic consequences
sub_criteria.description: Direct financial losses exceeding critical threshold, OR major disruption of one/multiple interdependent sectors
sub_criteria.note: Critical financial threshold defined by sectoral authorities; interdependent sector disruption evaluated via sector_dependencies.json

sub_criteria.label: National security
sub_criteria.description: Affects defence/intelligence/sensitive government systems, OR involves state actor/terrorist group/hybrid operation

sub_criteria.label: Sensitive data loss
sub_criteria.description: Exfiltration, destruction, or alteration of sensitive government data, industrial secrets, or critical strategic data

cooperation_modes.description: Distinction rests on whether prejudice is actual (Crise) or potential (Alerte/CERC)

modes.label: Crise
modes.condition: Event causes prejudice to vital interests

modes.label: Alerte/CERC
modes.condition: Event could cause prejudice to vital interests

fast_track.description: If the incident constitutes unauthorised access, suspected malicious, likely to cause grave operational disruptions — skip Criterion 2 entirely, proceed directly to Criterion 3 (coordination and urgency)
